findings.summary: Component counts correct: 16 total with 5 connectors, 4 fiducials, 2 resistors, 1 switch, 1 jumper, 3 other; I2C bus not detected despite R1/R2 (2.2k) pullup resistors on IO4/SDA and IO5/SCL nets; ...

correct.description: The Pico Add-On Flipper board is a pass-through PCB with 4x 20-pin SMD connectors (J1-J4), a Qwiic I2C connector (J5), two 2.2k I2C pullup resistors (R1, R2), a 3-way I2C jumper (JP1), a reset switch (SW1), and 4 fiducials plus 2 logos. The analyzer correctly identifies all 16 components with no DNP parts.

The test_coverage section correctly identifies IO4{slash}SDA and IO5{slash}SCL as uncovered_key_nets with category 'i2c', and VBUS/VSYS as power_rail. The observation 'Key nets without test points: 2 i2c, 2 power_rail' accurately reflects the absence of test points on this passthrough board.

missed.description: The bus_analysis.i2c is empty ([]) and design_observations is empty. The schematic has net names 'IO4{slash}SDA' and 'IO5{slash}SCL', with R2 pin 1 on IO4/SDA and R1 pin 1 on IO5/SCL, both pins 2 on 3.3V. These are classic I2C pullup resistors. The detection fails because the net names contain the IO prefix and slash encoding ('IO4{slash}SDA') rather than bare 'SDA'/'SCL'. The analyzer should match I2C lines from net names containing 'SDA' or 'SCL' as substrings, not just exact matches.

R1 (2.2k) has pin 2 on 3.3V and pin 1 on IO5/SCL, and R2 (2.2k) has pin 2 on 3.3V and pin 1 on IO4/SDA. These are textbook I2C pullup resistors connected directly from 3.3V to the bus lines. Because the bus names use compound net names (IO4{slash}SDA), the I2C detector fails to recognize these resistors as pullups, leaving design_observations empty and both decoupling_analysis and rc_filters empty.

findings.summary: PCB footprint count of 109 correctly includes 92 kibuzzard decorative footprints; Board dimensions are null despite Edge.Cuts layer being present in the PCB file; PCB routing complete with correct ...

correct.description: The PCB has 109 footprints total, with 92 kibuzzard decorative graphics (board_only) and 17 real components. This matches the schematic's 16 components plus 1 OSHW logo (G3) marked board-only in the PCB. The component_groups correctly lists 4 FID, 4 J, 1 JP, 2 R, 1 SW, and 4 G references alongside 92 kibuzzard refs.

The PCB is correctly identified as 2-layer (F.Cu, B.Cu) with routing_complete: true, 212 track segments, 43 vias, and 1 copper pour zone. Net count of 37 matches the schematic's 37 nets. The 0 THT components is correct as all real components (4x 20-pin SMD connectors, Qwiic JST, SMD resistors, SMD switch) are surface-mount.

incorrect.description: The statistics show board_width_mm: null and board_height_mm: null, and the board_outline section has edge_count: 0 and bounding_box: null. However, the PCB file does have an Edge.Cuts layer. This indicates the board outline is likely defined as a footprint reference rather than direct Edge.Cuts graphics, or uses a non-standard format, causing the analyzer to fail to extract the board boundary. The PCB is a known rectangular board for Raspberry Pi Pico passthrough.
